title: Navigation History, Back Semantics, Snapshots, and Failure Recovery
description: Back recomputes offsets instead of restoring observed state, failed requests corrupt history, repeated pages grow memory forever, and concurrent prefetch mutates the visible cursor. Learners cumulatively produce bounded snapshot navigation history through theory, guided work, debugging, transfer, retrieval, and assessment.
objectives: Define whether back means prior successful view, API previous link, or numeric page and expose that contract in help.
Store immutable-enough page snapshots or identifiers with explicit copy boundaries so later mutation cannot rewrite history.
Push history only after a new view is admitted and leave cursor plus history unchanged on failure or cancellation.
